preamble: The voiced uvular fricative /ʁ/, a common realization of the 'r' sound in French, can undergo a phonetic transformation to a voiced alveolar flap /ɾ/ when borrowed into certain Arabic dialects. This adaptation involves a shift in both the place and manner of articulation.
phoneticEffects: Place assimilation (uvular to alveolar), Manner change (fricative to flap), Lenition (fricative to flap)
sources: Sakr, G. (2017). A Discussion of Issues from French Loans in Lebanese.
Youssef, A. (2019). The phonology and micro-typology of Arabic R. Glossa: a journal of general linguistics, 4(1).
Lahrouchi, M. (2018). Not as you R: Adapting the French r into Arabic and Berber.
